skill_guidance_gaps: Skill mentions context managers for resource cleanup but does not show how to apply them in an order/payment context — not applied
Skill mentions functools.partial and functools.cache but gives no domain example — not applied
Skill does not mention singledispatch explicitly in the Quick Reference table; it is buried in the functools bullet — still applied because it was the cleanest fit for payment dispatch
Skill does not address module organisation (file layout) — added as extra structure to make the response useful
Skill Quick Reference table omits TypedDict and Protocols entirely; the old version lacks guidance on when to prefer Protocol vs ABC
code_correctness_notes: All patterns are syntactically valid Python 3.11+
StrEnum requires Python 3.11 (or enum.StrEnum backport for 3.10)
singledispatch on dataclass types works correctly because each payload class is distinct
Frozen dataclasses used consistently; no mutable domain objects
